mission.title: Install and Configure Containerssh on Kubernetes
mission.description: ContainerSSH is an SSH server that launches containers on demand in Kubernetes and Docker, providing dynamic access for various use cases such as lab environments, debugging, and honeypots. Installing ContainerSSH allows you to manage containerized environments efficiently and securely.

steps.title: Download ContainerSSH binary
steps.description:
Download the latest ContainerSSH release:
```bash
export CONTAINERSSH_VERSION=0.5.2
curl -Lo containerssh.tar.gz https://github.com/containerssh/containerssh/releases/download/v${CONTAINERSSH_VERSION}/containerssh_${CONTAINERSSH_VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz
tar xzf containerssh.tar.gz
sudo mv containerssh /usr/local/bin/
rm containerssh.tar.gz
```
Or use Docker:
```bash
docker pull containerssh/containerssh:0.5.2
```

steps.title: Configure ContainerSSH
steps.description:
Create the configuration file:
```bash
sudo mkdir -p /etc/containerssh
sudo tee /etc/containerssh/config.yaml << 'EOF'
ssh:
  listen: 0.0.0.0:2222
  hostkeys:
    - /etc/containerssh/ssh_host_rsa_key
auth:
  url: http://localhost:8080
backend: docker
docker:
  connection:
    host: unix:///var/run/docker.sock
EOF
sudo ssh-keygen -t rsa -f /etc/containerssh/ssh_host_rsa_key -N ''
```

steps.title: Start ContainerSSH
steps.description:
Run ContainerSSH:
```bash
containerssh --config /etc/containerssh/config.yaml
```
Or via Docker:
```bash
docker run -d --name containerssh -p 2222:2222 \
  -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \
  containerssh/containerssh:0.5.2
```

steps.title: Test SSH access
steps.description:
Connect via SSH:
```bash
ssh -p 2222 user@localhost
```
Each SSH session launches a new container.

uninstall.title: Remove ContainerSSH
uninstall.description:
```bash
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/containerssh
sudo rm -rf /etc/containerssh
# Or: docker stop containerssh && docker rm containerssh
```

upgrade.title: Download latest release
upgrade.description:
```bash
curl -Lo containerssh.tar.gz https://github.com/containerssh/containerssh/releases/latest/download/containerssh_linux_amd64.tar.gz
tar xzf containerssh.tar.gz
sudo mv containerssh /usr/local/bin/
```

troubleshooting.title: SSH connection refused
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure ContainerSSH is running and listening on port 2222:
```bash
ss -tlnp | grep 2222
```

troubleshooting.title: Authentication webhook unreachable
troubleshooting.description:
ContainerSSH requires an auth webhook. Use the test server for development:
```bash
docker run -d -p 8080:8080 containerssh/containerssh-test-authconfig-server:0.5.2
```

troubleshooting.title: Container launch failures
troubleshooting.description:
Check that Docker is running and accessible:
```bash
docker ps
```

resolution.summary: ContainerSSH is installed and provides SSH access that launches isolated containers for each session.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a working Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with it.
